review_text: Unit had everything we needed for our 2 night stay recently.  Fully renovated and excellent inside. Loved the big brand new top of the range BBQ. Staff were wonderfully helpful and knowledgeable.  Excellent value for money. Comfy new q bed. Would definitely stay here again.

Jo Anne and Andrew are the owners , they are the most fairest, kindest , honest and some of most reasonable people I've ever met, Simply Brilliant !!! at this Limestone Coast Tourist Park, you couldn't get any fairer people than both of these owner/ operators, I would give them 6 Stars, if I could actually, quite frankly, if there was, such a higher level !! , It's not just the rooms, the people who run this place, Rubina is a worker and is  excellent also cando attitude. The rooms and units are first class, as far as I'm concerned, very well priced and have keep the great Australian values which is so important, the Customer Service is second to None , just very well managed , Andrew and Jo Anne are both good, conservative people , just do anything for you, anything extra in needs or fixing is done , no hesitations, no fuss , just done !!!simply easy and helpful, the way it should be. Who doesn't want that?? Exactly , no one, best way to have it, If you think it's too good to be true, experience this place for yourself ?  You WON'T be disappointed, you will be probably just surprised that places, like this,  still exist these days, extremely rare phenomena, more than  anything, try finding another place like this , you probably wouldn't !!!🤚I can't fault the place and certainly not Jo Anne and Andrew, this is what separates them from the rest , they are both figures of 8 ahead. Until you stay here , it's a bit hard to understand , then you will understand and see, what I'm talking about and everyone else I've spoken too also not rocket science haha lol !!!! Enjoy your Stay !!!!
Bless them all.
Kindest Regards Always,
Jarrad D. 🙏🤚👍
Enjoy staying here, very clean solid rooms, they've put effort into doing the rooms and kitchen up, and has great sunsets looking over Mt Gambier.
Handy

Absolutely Amazing the customer service was so helpful, value for money and i was blown away with the room so spacious and comfortable but so spotless.
AAA rating on service,room and price
Peaceful, friendly caravan park. Clean rooms, comfortable beds. We'll be back.
Excellent room for the price.

Joanne was an amazing host — her communication was excellent from start to finish. She went above and beyond to help us extend our stay and even offered a very generous 50% discount, which we truly appreciated. You can really feel that this is a warm, family-run business.

The rooms were cozy, clean, and very comfortable. The park itself has great facilities, including BBQ areas, a fireplace, and a fantastic kids’ play area — my kids absolutely loved it and didn’t want to leave. We stayed for 3 nights and had a really great experience. Highly recommend this place for families and anyone looking for a welcoming and relaxing stay.

Joanne and Andrew were super helpful and made sure our stay was as comfortable as possible. They were willing to accomodate our needs after hours and exceeded our expectations. Ensuite bathroom was clean and functional and other facilities were a treat.
Thanks Andrew and Joanne for the lovely stay!
Very nice location and good facilities and friendly staff. Recommend 2 bedroom accommodation
Amazing people running this place! We had car troubles and they were kind enough to help us sort the problem without hesitation

My friend booked a night for group of 12.
12 of of us got bed. They arranged rooms very well and affordable. Plenty of sealed blankets, towels, bedsheets. Everything was super clean. The motel look after us very well.
We really enjoyed our stay. Receptionist was friendly with kids providing the soccer balls.
Our previous stay at presidential motel was super expensive comparing limestone is far better.

Not happy with our stay here at $200 a night, booked for 3 nights as we had a funeral and are from interstate.. left after one night, lost our $400 as office closed all day on Sunday so not able to discuss our situation, it was our choice to leave as we found a nicer place! The area at the front for people with their own vans was nice to arrive at but up the back where we were was far from that. Fortunately we found a beautiful property called 'Pet Friendly Cottages' (they had a cancellation for two nights) in Worrolong which was just perfect in every way, the whole property was so inviting, heater on for us on arrival, very cosy for us and our dog Monty. Goodies in the house that we enjoyed,  owners Russell and Pauline.. so so friendly you couldn't have asked for any more. Definately dog friendly and you couldn't compare to where we first booked and stayed. We found it via my sister in law who lives in Mt Gambier. Open space, country feel, small lockable yard for Monty the dog to be in and safe.The cabin (Limestone Coast) under this review WAS clean, bed comfy with electric blanket, but cold and uninviting. We booked 3 nights, stayed one and lost $400. Charged $200 a night but looking now on website it says $133.00 a night..not sure why extra cost, maybe because of the rodeo and sporting weekend there,   all dog friendly was booked out! We were up the back of the park in a cabin no. 5. We had a small dog. It wasn't pet friendly, it was pets welcome!!! There was no yard or area at all for a dog. We were up the back where it was quite unkept. What little lawn there was, was unmowed. It was 4 rooms in a square box, website deceiving, looked much nicer and bigger than ours was. A small porch, a lounge come bedroom/double bunk/couch/tv room, all in one. No cupboards or shelves in the bathroom to put essentials in or on, so all items on the floor. No wardrobe, a small unit on the wall with 2 coat hangers for our clothes, that was it had to live out of our cases that could only go on the bunks in the lounge/bedroom. Kitchen small but usable, all utensils and crockery old and well used. Shower awful, no water pressure in the shower or hot tap over the sink. Shower water ran hot and cold, hard to adjust then just went cold. There was no lighting outside our cabin so thank goodness we had a torch to take the dog for a walk before his bedtime. I saw a couple of reviews and wondered if it was the same part of the park we were in. We have no caravan to travel with, so rely on the website to be accurate. We've travelled a lot, over the years and mostly with a dog but never not stayed in the places we had booked on line.  Unfortunately this isn't a nice place to have stayed and i think their ratings for some or all the cabins up the back should be reviewed....

Absolutely fantastic, thanks to Joanne for helping us out at short notice and making me feel comfortable and relaxed. Our 3 bedroom unit was perfect and the shower pressure amazing, 100% eased the tension I was feeling. Thank you , we will be back if we come through Mt Gambier again.

Located just out of town our pet friendly cabin was suitable for its purpose , has a fairly good kitchen with the basics you would expect, the charges are reasonable for a pet friendly cabin , bed is soft and could be upgraded but bearable. Toilet and shower was clean and worked ok . Welcoming staff and most people keep their dogs on leash which is expected.
Thanks for having us 😀🐾🐶

A little out of city centre but quiet.
Has cooking facilities and rooms were clean.
Absolutely loved this cabin we stayed in. Pet friendly (deluxe quadruple room).
Such lovely and genuinely kind service provided at reception! Thank you very much for taking care of us during our stay! :)
Very friendly and helpful owners.
We enjoyed staying here. Clean ensuite, yard workers getting on top of grass. Quiet and friendly.
Lovely friendly staff.

Our cabin was an actual building, not a mobile-style one. I think most here are like that. This meant it was quite large and had a separate kitchen and entry area. Decor and furnishings are not modern but it was clean, comfortable and had everything you might need, including a big fridge, well-equipped kitchen and large tv. Our dogs were also welcome, although it was hard keeping them just in the alcove and kitchen in winter as the heating was more effective with the kitchen door closed. Now there is modern laminate flooring in the living area, perhaps management may revise this condition? There is a large, fully fenced off lead area, that was very useful for dogs to run after being in the car. Staff were helpful and friendly.

This park was chosen because of the price and after staying there it's clear.  Our van site was not flat but on a slight decline.  We did have a private ensuite but it looked like it hadn't been cleaned in years.  There were a lot of permanent residents in the park as well.
Everything was great! Will be back !
Have stayed a couple different places and this has been the best one. Dog friendly with their own off leash section at the bottom. Very friendly and helpful staff. Will be back next year.

One of the worst customer service ever. We were travelling with our old parents and the heater in the room wasn’t working at all. We asked Andrew to help us with extra electric blanket or a small heater. Instead of helping us out he passed couple of RACIST COMMENTS saying this country is not meant for us and we should go back to Asia. He said FOUR TIMES you shouldnt be in this country.
We would never come to this place. PLEASE DO NOT DO ANY BOOKING HERE YOU WILL ONLY BE DISAPPOINTED.

We stayed here for the first time over Easter. The cabin was a good size and clean and comfortable. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The location was good for us as it is just on the outskirts of the city and in a quiet location with a good view. The price of the cabin was reasonable and would have no hesitation staying there again in the future.

We stayed at limestone coast caravan park for 2 nights at a ensuit site. The bathroom was spotless we wanted a late checkout and without any problems they let us. The location was perfect on the main road and near the city centre. If we ever need to go past mount gambier definitely limestone coast caravan park will be the place to stop over.

Stayed 3 night on a powered site with ensuite. Although they have rooms as well, I think there are only 10 powered sites.
Although dated, ensuite was clean and spacious.
Used as a base to see the local attractions. Only 2ks from Umpherston sink hole and maybe 7-8 minutes to town. Plenty to see in the area.
Park is elevated and we had a good view, particularly the sunset which for those into that sort of thing, was pretty reasonable I thought.
We paid $40/night for power, water, ensuite, security and proximity to everything we needed.

What a place! Andrew, Joanna, Dave! You guys rock! So friendly and helpful. The park itself has great facilities including massive camp kitchen, laundry, And it's own OFF LEAD DOG PARK ONSITE! how cool is that! Will definitely be back when we come this way again

I booked a suite at this place via Booking.com on February 15th and received an automated email confirming my reservation. On the same day, I also contacted the property using the number provided, and they confirmed my booking again.

However, on February 26th, I received a call from them informing me that the suite was no longer available and advising me to find another place.

When I checked online, there were no available suites that met our requirements at that time, and there were no other suitable places nearby.

As a result, our trip was completely ruined by this incident.

I’ll leave the rest for you to decide.
Very clean great service.
Great people who run the place. Happy and accommodating.

Limestone Coast Tourist Park|Hi|Well you would have to say it’s past its prime. We had an ensuite powered caravan site and after the less than friendly welcome we then had to deal with an uneven site … for a park of this age …level should be normal.|Simply|#1… if the wind is south west the pong from the Jubilee sawmill is really off (treatment of pine logs)|#2 … Sunday morning … the wail from the Jubilee Christian Centre next door will wake you and drive you bonkers|#3 … have a thick skin… the curmudgeon will be a hassle

I absouletly loved my time here. (Jan 29th-Feb 8th 2025)
The staff were lovely. The cabin comfortable and spacious. My puppy Alfie, a cavoodle  was able to stay and they also have a large off leash dog park on site for your doggo to run free. I was given a free night stay after I was having to keep extending my stay after my car was involved in an insurance issue the day we drove up. The owner was incredibly accommodating. I had great air-conditioning,  a large and small fridge, microwave and all the basic amenities i needed. I promise you, I will be returning in the future. Bonus, I was close to all shopping centres. I also just recommend you take extra large tarp to cover your car over night. The birdies love playing 💩 target with your car in the mornings. 😆 highly recommend this place. So peaceful.
Thank you for your hospitality, kindness and understanding. 🤩 uploaded the view from my cabin window of an evening. Photo of my puppy heading down to the off leash doggo Area. Bliss.
I have lived here for 5 months so beautiful and peaceful I have my own toilet and shower by my caravan and I have dogs .this park is super pet friendly 😍.
Staff were absolutely lovely and accommodating. Great prices for a powered site with a private ensuite!

I stayed in deluxe cabin during Christmas 24. Location is really good .I  know park is pet friendly  but there were dog hair on floor, couch and  tiles.  My daughter is 9 months old and she love to crawl on floor which made hard for us to let her stay on floor to play. A cupboard for cloths would be handy and worst thing was, toilets window opening in kitchen. So overall a decent place if you don't mind dog hair all over the place.
Clean and tidy house
Great place, friendly owner.
Had a site for my motorhome. All was good.
Overall is ok but don't have hot water for shower

Stayed here for 2 nights. Looks were already as deceiving. There was no Wifi, wouldn't recommend this to people who enjoy video games. No kitchen sink, had to drink coffee from the bathroom sink, the countryside view was amazing, Lots of annoying bugs. Won't come again
Very clean, good location

We booked 4 cabins. Awesome experience. Rooms were nice n clean and specious. Everything needed was provided in the room. Bbq area was perfect. We were lucky to use the brand new bbq units which were just installed. Location is great. Close to all sinkholes n markets. Will be staying here again for sure. Nice people and understanding. Thanks Joanne for making our stat great.

STOP! DO NOT stay there! Worst place I ever stayed at. We were there 3 months, November 2023 to February 2024. We had to purchase our own mower, just to keep the grass down, sit gingerly on the multiple cracked toilet, (in fear it was going to break), get zapped while drying my hair. ||Then there is the steep and unsteady roads to lead you to your site.... Massive, overhanging trees, which hooked, (through the winds) and ripped down our antenna!||This place is certainly not suited for children!||What is worse, it the smell of alcohol, on the breath of the owner, And the attitude of them! My daughter visited while there and slipped on the embankment. She was obviously hurt, so we rushed her through to Mt Gambier hospital, where she had torn her ankle tendons. The response by the owner... f..k off. Get F..ked, get out of my office!   WE LEFT!||What else we wittnessed whilst staying there, would curl your toe nails!||PLUS, I am sure also, the taxation department would benefit by Auditing them!

Very disappointing.  Room was dusty. Bbq discusting very very dirty .Rude staff. And dirty bed linen.  Won't be going here again

Haha I'm using my nickname buddy. Oh we were there it was filthy look at the rest of your reviews.  You were at reception and you were a very rude old man . Your wife and family are in QLD hence yr vans rego.  Clean up the place and you would have better reviews
Whatever you do, don't make arrangements with them for rental days thry bang on your windows like police looking for money earlier than agreed upon

We have stayed here several times. The management are really friendly and very helpful. We have stayed on the campsites and in the cabins and have been happy with both. The sites have ensuite bathrooms which we love as they are so convenient to use. No bathroom queues!
It is a good caravan park The power sides are very good
Andrew great host and great conversation 😊
Very nice place

What a lovely hidden Jem very friendly staff and they welcomed our puppy the room was very clean and comfy highly reccomend if you coming with a pet they have a dog friendly section for them to run around it’s close to everything and has great dog friendly rooms

Booked same day as arriving. Jo was extremely helpful & polite, accommodating myself, 15 yr old son & our Chihuahua.
The room was exceptionally clean, comfortable with double bed & bunks in open room with couch & tv.
Full size cooking facilities & fridge. Exactlly what we needed. Couch was super comfy.
Slightly older, but updated & fresh. It's accommodation ur furbaby is welcome.
We arrived after reception was closed & Andrew was friendly & helpful.
Park itself was peaceful, beautiful gums, leash-free dog area, games room & camp kitchen.
If ur travelling with ur furbaby, highly recommend staying here.
Would give 10 stars if poss. Thanku Jo & Andrew for exceptional service
A very good place to stay. Excellent en suite. Helpful staff
Very nice and beautiful location just minutes away from all the major attractions around.
Cheap and b St of you are visiting mount Gambeir

Old property but very clean.
Owners are very helpful.
Nice BBQ & outdoor fire place.
Clean but bit outdated to my liking.

I found staff  pleasant and helpful on arrival. The room has everything you need and more, beds clean and very comfortable. I had my dog with me, and she was happy to have the time in the off leash area. I would stay again. The views are fantastic. Being of the highway, heard no traffic.

The rooms are tidy the owner is a really nice guy
Park is old but well maintained
Good place for a family holiday

People owning this place are very kind.
But the rooms are bit old, carpet is bit smelly, taps in the bathroom were leaking, the leather couches had spaces in b/W the seats, my phone went underneath twice. The night lights in the bedroom are just at the back of the bedhead, too scary to hit the big globes with hands or head while sleeping. Also you can’t sit on the bed because of those lights.
The heater was only in the living room not the bedrooms, luckily there were electric blankets

Exceptionally clean rooms. they had everything we needed for a 2 night stay. all the cutlery provided was clean as well. it's not tooo far from the city which is great. the community kitchen had all the utensils for cooking food. the TT, air hockey and the billiards table was a huge plus. we had a great time. the limited interaction that we had with the owners was fine. no complaints, will visit again. recommended.

We stayed 2 nights, terrific. Both of us having mobility issues, Joanne assured us that we would be comfortable in cabin 7. And we were very comfortable. Joanne & Andrew are working their way through cabins doing them up. Yes, there is age on the property, you can't help that. And no you can't make a silk purse out of a sows ear.BUT!!!! Our cabin, was very clean, bed unreal comfy, and those pillows?OMG, I took a photo of the brand. 1st night ever, that I wasn't kept awake by partners snoring. Towels lovely, kitchen has absolutely everything that you could ask for,and a full size fridge. You could stay for weeks. Quiet area, we really enjoyed ourselves. Thank you Joanne &Andrew. As for travellers? Forget about motel rooms where you step on each other, this is the way to go.

Stayed for one night traveling threw.
Room was comfortable.

The room was very clean. New microwave. Stove was super clean. Requested to change the room for queen sized beds and the lady on the phone (sorry I forgot the name) tried every possible way and got us what we requested.

Only minor issue was you can hear people talking from the unit next to you, specially at night when ur trying to sleep. This can be easily fixed by soundproof material.

Overall all good.  I’ll definitely stay again.
Ensuite bathroom a falls risk due to 2 leaks and wet floor. In need of an update.
Good site with average ensuite I.e. wet floor from weather blowing under door & water leaks. Couldn't find dump point that was listed.
Amazing hosts.
Run away

Stayed here for a few nights. The lady who took my booking was really nice and helpful. The man not so much! Was very abrupt on arrival, then Kids playing in the games room accidentally dropped a pool ball. He come in and growled so I was packing all balls up so kids wouldn’t touch them anymore he come racing in telling me I am lying when I told him we didn’t drop anymore balls. I was trying to tell him we’d collected them all to put them out of kids reach he Carried on to us how they are not Chinese rubbish and they cost him $200,  well honestly why put something so expensive in a games room that children will be in? So we didn’t feel comfortable doing anything else at the park. We tried to stay away as much as possible.

The bbq facilities were disgusting. Needed a very good makeover and a decent clean up.
The playground has a very nasty fall hazard not many meters away from it. Really needs a fence around it.
The rooms were ok. Ovens didn’t work real well so waisted a roast trying to cook it.
Stove was good though.

Shower pressure that good we couldn’t keep the water in the shower properly and the shower curtain doesn’t do anything. No toiletries and no room service (which it does say in the main page I think) so we had to go and buy towels.

We moved the bed to pick up a toy that had gone under there. Anyone else don’t look under there. They were not clean at all. Also there is mould coming up in places in the room. Mainly the roof and the toilet seat.
And the toilet paper is only one ply haha!!

Very cheap accomodation and I can see why…

Dog park was good tho.

Very outdated park needs a good makeover.

Endroit au top lors d’un arrêt avec un campervan. Douches individuelles (!). Cuisine super équipées.
Personnel agréable

Overall this place is very good.

The rooms have a bit of a strange layout but they are very comfortable for a solo business trip to the area.

The wifi isn't that good but the hotspot worked fine there.

This kitchen is stocked with pretty much everything you need, the beds were very comfortable and there is a nice couch/tv set up

The staff are unbelievably friendly and went out of their way for me, which I am very thankful for.

McDonald's and a pub is near by if you are feeling lazy for dinner.
The renovated pink row cabins with ensuite are very nice and the service is great.

We stayed here for 1 night. While the room was clean and tidy, there was a large group staying right behind our cabin who partied outdoors till 3am! No staff member was on site to put a stop to it, resulting in us not getting much sleep due to all the racket. When I complained to Andrew the next morning, he simply shrugged his shoulders.
